differential_diagnoses: PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Hidradenitis suppurativa)
Crohn's disease with systemic extraintestinal manifestations
next_steps: Perform punch biopsy of the advancing edge of an ulcer for histopathology, tissue culture, and Gram/AFB/fungal stains
Initiate systemic glucocorticoid therapy (e.g., high-dose intravenous methylprednisolone) as the presumptive treatment for severe neutrophilic dermatosis
Consult Dermatology and Rheumatology immediately for specialized management
Perform ileocolonoscopy to evaluate for occult Crohn's disease given family history and episodic GI symptoms
Perform repeat echocardiography and potentially cardiac MRI to further evaluate the right-sided cardiac strain and pulmonary artery dilation
Careful bedside wound management avoiding unnecessary surgical debridement to prevent exacerbation of pathergy
